raw_response:
I agree that Option A is correct because the length of enamel rods does not necessarily match their overall thickness; this can vary depending on location within the enamel structure. Additionally, enamel rods are indeed narrower near the dentino-enamel junction (DEJ) and wider towards the outer surfaces of the teeth, which supports B being accurate but further confirms A’s inaccuracy since these two statements describe different aspects of the same structural feature.

My answer: **A**.
